title: BUbuisnessAndMarket
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbuisnessAndMarket, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: Walmart exec says it’s ‘unfortunate’ that other companies are slashing workforces in the name of AI—they’re offering training to 1.6 million workers instead
items.description: Trillion-dollar retail giant Walmart says it’s not using AI to slash headcounts—instead, it’s providing workers with free access to Google AI training...

items.title: Just 5% of workers have this skill, according to Google—and they’re 4.5x times more likely to have a higher salary
items.description: The C-suite is pushing for AI productivity gains, but less than half of all workers are using the technology—or getting the proper training, according...

items.title: Royal Caribbean’s CEO is using AI to predict how many hamburgers you’ll eat—and to compete with Orlando, Vegas and Taylor Swift
items.description: "We're massive users of AI in a non-creepy way," Royal Caribbean CEO Jason Liberty told Fortune.

items.title: Amazon’s humble history: Jeff Bezos used a rented garage as an office and held team meetings at a local Barnes & Noble
items.description: Amazon's trajectory from a rented garage to a $2.4 trillion enterprise is now business legend.

items.title: AI is running out of power. Space won’t be an escape hatch for decades
items.description: The physics mostly work. The timelines, economics, and infrastructure don’t—at least not yet.
